info.name: ActOne Extend REST APIs
info.description: Hand-built collection for ActOne: Login (captures CSRF token + session cookie) followed by Save Step Changes. Base URL is configured via the {{rcm}} environment variable (e.g. http://10.233.194.40:8080/RCM).

exec: // Capture CSRF token from the response header and store it for later requests

request.description:
POST /RCM/api/public/v1/auth/login

Logs in with username/password. A successful login (200) returns a session cookie (stored automatically in Postman's cookie jar) and a CSRFTOKEN response header (captured into the {{CSRFTOKEN}} environment variable by the test script).

exec: // Tomcat rejects raw { } [ ] " in the URL ('Invalid character in request target').
// Pre-encode the JSON-valued query params so the runtime sends valid percent-encoding.

formdata.description: Placeholder so Postman sends a valid multipart/form-data body. Real file parts go here as 'files' when attaching attachments.

query.description: Required. Must be valid JSON: a JSON array of identifiers, e.g. ["id1","id2"]. The pre-request script builds this percent-encoded into {{wi_q}} from work_item_id, because Tomcat rejects raw [ ] " in the URL.

Target workflow status identifier (the step IDENTIFIER, e.g. Under_QA_Review_2 / QA_Completed_3 — case sensitive), not the display name. Must be an allowed next step from the item's current step. Omit to leave the status unchanged.

Must be a JSON object with ONLY the 'note' key: {"note":"text"}. The pre-request script builds this percent-encoded into {{note_q}} from the 'note' env var. Required for steps whose requiresNote=true (e.g. Under_QA_Review_2).
Forces save step status update, ignoring normal workflow checks. Default false. Requires the 'Execute Web Service' permission.
JSON array of reason objects, e.g. [{"codeField":"...","nameField":"..."}].
Boolean flag ('true'/'false') marking the note as confidential. Defaults to false.
Boolean flag ('true'/'false') to reset the recall counter on the work item.

request.description:
POST /RCM/api/v1/work-items/workflow/save-step

All inputs are QUERY parameters. Authentication relies on the session cookie set by Login (sent automatically) plus the CSRFTOKEN header.

Two gotchas verified against the live server:
1. The endpoint ONLY accepts Content-Type 'multipart/form-data'. Sending none, application/json or application/x-www-form-urlencoded returns 'HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type'. The body is therefore set to form-data mode (Postman adds the multipart boundary automatically — do NOT set Content-Type manually). The empty '_' field just guarantees a valid multipart body; add real 'files' parts here to attach files.
2. workItemIdentifiers must be valid JSON — a JSON array like ["id1"] (hence value ["{{work_item_id}}"]). A bare id returns 400 'Bad JSON syntax'.

Set work_item_id in the environment to a real work item id; an invalid id returns 404 'Could not find Work Item'.
